summary: Plan-mode PR inventory classification. No shared canonical was selected because the job is an independent PR inventory shard. Security-linked refs were quarantined only where explicitly marked by the hydrated artifact; open PR candidates were classified conservatively for maintainer review, author follow-up, or already-closed state.

actions.reason: The PR is directly tied to an unauthenticated pre-auth CPU DoS report and should be routed to central OpenClaw security handling instead of backlog cleanup.
Maintainer-requested technical correctness judgment remains unresolved after author follow-up.
Already closed; no mutation should be planned.
Distinct open PR with insufficient proof; keep related for normal maintainer triage rather than close.
Focused candidate PR linked to an open issue, but maintainer/product review is still required; no close or merge action is allowed.
Focused open candidate for the corpus=all timeout root cause; keep related for maintainer review.
Large cross-surface behavior change with active author follow-up and unresolved review/maintainer judgment.
Open feature PR with missing proof; not low-signal or safely closable in this plan job.
Related focused PR with useful code and broader issue context; keep for maintainer review rather than close.
Focused open candidate with passing proof; keep related for maintainer review. Merge is blocked by job frontmatter.
Security-sensitive historical context must be routed/read-only, not used for cleanup mutation.
Open vulnerability-shaped issue belongs in central OpenClaw security handling, not PR inventory cleanup.

needs_human: #90490 requires maintainer technical correctness judgment after a CHANGES_REQUESTED review and author follow-up commits.
#75662 requires maintainer technical correctness judgment for a large session lifecycle change with unresolved review risk and active author follow-up.
